# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pytest
from lib.ansible.module_utils.redfish_utils import RedfishUtils

class _TestRedfishUtils(RedfishUtils):
    """Test double that avoids network and captures requested URIs."""

    def __init__(self, creds, root_uri):
        self.root_uri = root_uri
        self.creds = creds
        self.requested_uris = []

    def get_request(self, uri):
        self.requested_uris.append(uri)
        if uri.endswith('/redfish/v1/UpdateService'):
            return {'ret': True, 'data': {'Actions': {'#UpdateService.SimpleUpdate': {'title': 'SimpleUpdate', 'TransferProtocol@Redfish.AllowableValues': ['HTTPS']}}}}
        if uri.endswith('/redfish/v1'):
            return {'ret': True, 'data': {'UpdateService': {'@odata.id': '/redfish/v1/UpdateService'}}}
        return {'ret': False, 'msg': f'Unexpected URI requested: {uri}'}

def test_get_firmware_update_capabilities_uses_update_uri_not_hardcoded_service_root__a1():
    """
    Ensure get_firmware_update_capabilities does not hardcode '/redfish/v1',
    but instead uses the UpdateService URI (self.update_uri) discovered earlier.
    """
    rf = _TestRedfishUtils(creds={'user': 'u', 'pswd': 'p'}, root_uri='https://example.invalid')
    rf.update_uri = '/redfish/v1/UpdateService'
    result = rf.get_firmware_update_capabilities()
    assert result.get('ret') is True, f'Expected successful result, got: {result}'

def test_get_firmware_update_capabilities_uses_update_uri_not_hardcoded_service_root__a2():
    """
    Ensure get_firmware_update_capabilities does not hardcode '/redfish/v1',
    but instead uses the UpdateService URI (self.update_uri) discovered earlier.
    """
    rf = _TestRedfishUtils(creds={'user': 'u', 'pswd': 'p'}, root_uri='https://example.invalid')
    rf.update_uri = '/redfish/v1/UpdateService'
    result = rf.get_firmware_update_capabilities()
    assert rf.requested_uris, 'Expected at least one GET request to be made'

def test_get_firmware_update_capabilities_uses_update_uri_not_hardcoded_service_root__a3():
    """
    Ensure get_firmware_update_capabilities does not hardcode '/redfish/v1',
    but instead uses the UpdateService URI (self.update_uri) discovered earlier.
    """
    rf = _TestRedfishUtils(creds={'user': 'u', 'pswd': 'p'}, root_uri='https://example.invalid')
    rf.update_uri = '/redfish/v1/UpdateService'
    result = rf.get_firmware_update_capabilities()
    first_uri = rf.requested_uris[0]
    assert first_uri.endswith(rf.update_uri), f"Expected get_firmware_update_capabilities() to call get_request() with root_uri + self.update_uri as the first request (discovered UpdateService URI), but first requested URI was: {first_uri!r}. This indicates a hardcoded '/redfish/v1' service root is being used instead of the module-provided UpdateService URI."

def test_get_firmware_update_capabilities_uses_update_uri_not_hardcoded_service_root__a4():
    """
    Ensure get_firmware_update_capabilities does not hardcode '/redfish/v1',
    but instead uses the UpdateService URI (self.update_uri) discovered earlier.
    """
    rf = _TestRedfishUtils(creds={'user': 'u', 'pswd': 'p'}, root_uri='https://example.invalid')
    rf.update_uri = '/redfish/v1/UpdateService'
    result = rf.get_firmware_update_capabilities()
    first_uri = rf.requested_uris[0]
    assert not first_uri.endswith('/redfish/v1'), "get_firmware_update_capabilities() should not hardcode the Redfish service root ('/redfish/v1') when update_uri is available; it should query UpdateService directly."
